questions.prompt: Direct: "We have already finished the project," they said. Choose the correct reported form:

options.text: They said they had already finished the project.
They said they have already finished the project.
They told they had already finished the project.
They said they finished already the project.

questions.explanation: Present perfect ('have finished') backshifts to past perfect ('had finished'). 'We' changes to 'they'. We use 'say (that)', not 'told (that)'.

questions.prompt: Direct: "Where does she live?" he asked. Choose the correct reported question:

options.text: He asked where does she live.
He asked where she lives.
He asked where did she live.
He asked where she lived.

questions.explanation: Reported wh-questions use normal word order (no inversion, no auxiliary) and apply backshift: 'does she live' → 'she lived'. No question mark in reported speech.

questions.prompt: Direct: "I studied here yesterday," she told me. Identify all the changes in the reported form: 'She told me she had studied there the day before.'

options.text: Only the tense changed.
Only the pronoun and time word changed.
The pronoun, tense, place word, and time word all changed.
The tense, place word, and time word all changed.

questions.explanation: 'I' → 'she' (pronoun); past simple 'studied' → past perfect 'had studied' (tense backshift); 'here' → 'there' (place word); 'yesterday' → 'the day before' (time word). All four elements changed.

questions.prompt: Direct: "Can you open the window?" she asked him. Choose the correct reported form:

options.text: She asked him if he can open the window.
She told him if he could open the window.
She asked him if he could open the window.
She asked him could he open the window.

questions.explanation: A yes/no question is reported with 'if/whether'. 'can' backshifts to 'could'. Normal word order: 'he could open'. The pronoun 'you' changes to 'he'.

questions.prompt: Direct: "Come back tomorrow!" he told her. Choose the correct reported command:

options.text: He told her to come back the next day.
He told her to come back tomorrow.
He said her to come back the next day.
He told to come back the next day.

questions.explanation: Commands use 'tell + person + to + infinitive'. The time word 'tomorrow' shifts to 'the next day' in reported speech. 'said her' is wrong — 'say' never takes a direct person object.

questions.prompt: Which sentence applies the WRONG backshift?

options.text: He said he would call me. (will → would)
They said they can finish. (can → no change)
She said she had left early. (past simple → past perfect)
He said he was studying. (present continuous → past continuous)

questions.explanation: When the reporting verb is in the past, 'can' must backshift to 'could'. Saying 'they can finish' is incorrect — it should be 'they could finish'.
